text: I find myself transitioning from a sterile, glass-walled meeting room into a curated exhibit of ideas and intentionality. The first space felt cold, almost utilitarian—the kind of room where human connection is more a requirement than a desire. Stepping into the R+D space, I sense an invitation to participate in something beyond simple administration: the celebration of research, growth, and possibility. Reflecting on these contrasting environments, I experience a gentle hope—wondering how structured spaces can incubate both imagination and progress, if only we allow ourselves moments of reflection within them.
